briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Jul 22 Mag 7 earnings split the AI tape — Alphabet cloud +82% but FY26 capex raised to $195–205bn (stock −4% AMC) while Tesla revenue +26% missed on opex +47% and negative FCF (stock −5% AMC). Brent settled $94.07 (+3.36%) on Houthi Red Sea tanker hits; Jul 23 Asia Brent intraday ~$98.59 (+4.8%, 第一财经). Jul 23 China official close: Shanghai +0.25% to 3876.78 with >4200 stocks up but semis (华虹 −12%+) and STAR50 −3.78% lag — grid/oil/resources led. 30y held ~5.14% for a record 12th day above 5% (Bloomberg chart ~28 YTD days >5%).

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Jul 22 Asia split: A-share profit-taking (Shanghai +0.07%, ChiNext −3.23%) and Hang Seng −0.95% / HSTech −3.04% after Jul 21 chip V-reversal; Japan Jun trade deficit ¥406.9bn missed est ¥120bn; UK CPI 2.6% missed est 2.7%. Jul 21 US chip rebound (S&P +0.89%) and Brent $91.01 settle still anchor oil/geo inflation into Alphabet/Tesla Jul 22 earnings.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Jul 21 A-share full-day rally (Shanghai +1.79%, ChiNext +7.05%, turnover 2.96tn; semi deep-V per 第一财经) diverges from Jul 20 US risk-off (S&P −0.19%, Brent $89.22). Same-day Asia/UK prints: Taiwan Jun export orders +59.4% y/y beat estimate (47.3%); HK Jun CPI +2.0% y/y miss estimate (2.2%); UK May ILO unemployment 4.9% miss estimate (5.0%). Burnham/Healey fiscal “flexibility” pushed UK 10y gilts to 5.049%; Goldman PB record US tech de-grossing (彭博 Jul 21). Mag 7 earnings (Alphabet/Tesla Jul 22).

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Jul 20 Brent topped $90 after weekend CENTCOM strikes; Jul 17 US close stayed risk-off (S&P −1.01%, SOX bear market) and Jul 20 Kospi −4.46% extended the Kimi/semi rout. China state-team buying (>500亿 + ~100亿) and CSRC Jul 20 talks lifted Shanghai +0.85% to 3,796.28 and Hang Seng +2.36% to 25,143.05 despite STAR −2.28% and broad semis/PCB weakness.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Weekend US-Iran escalation — two US troops killed in Jordan, Kuwait desalination/oil hits, and Jul 19 CENTCOM airstrikes — landed after Jul 17 Brent settled $88.10 (+15.90% on the week) and SOX entered a bear market; equities/crypto closed Jul 17 risk-off (S&P −1.01%, Shanghai −3.05%).

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Jul 17 Kimi K3 reignited AI capex doubts — SOX entered a bear market (−1.6%, −20.2% from Jun 22 high) and Nasdaq −1.4%; Brent settled $88.10 (+4.6%) as CENTCOM's seventh night of Iran strikes collided with Shanghai −3.05% (broke 3800) on WAIC day.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Jul 16 chip profit-taking dragged Nasdaq −1.47% and SOX −4.3%; Jul 17 A-shares plunged (Shanghai −3.05% to 3764, broke 3800) as global semi beta overwhelmed WAIC headlines; Logan/Jefferson Fed split keeps September hike debate alive with Brent $84.23.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: TSMC Q2 beat and raised FY26 revenue >40% confirmed AI demand, but H2 margin compression and BOK's first hike in 3.5 years sent Kospi −6.37%; Williams (Jul 15) said inflation peaked while Jul 15 CPI/PPI both missed and Brent settled $84.95.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Double disinflation miss — June CPI and PPI both cool vs consensus, Kospi +6.2% and ASML raised FY26 sales again; Warsh's Senate Day-2 kept the hawkish filter alive while Brent settled $85.90 and China Q2 GDP missed at 4.3%.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: CPI miss eases July-hike panic, but Warsh says not 'mission accomplished' — S&P +0.4% and Nasdaq +0.9% close up while Brent settles at $84.73 and Iran blockade risk persists.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Risk-off — Middle East escalation reprices oil, inflation, and Fed policy; semiconductors under pressure despite AI narrative.
